title: Identification of allergenic proteins of Atlantic mackerel (Scomber scombrus) by real-time PCR method
author: Veterinary Scientific Committee
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The aim of this study was to develop the real-time PCR method for the detection of the partial sequence of gene encoding an allergenic protein parvalbumin of Atlantic mackerel (Scomber scombrus). This method enables to detect the short parts (100-150 bp heat or mecanically damaged DNA. The method is suitable even for the highly sensitive detection of several DNA  
molecules in the sample. The investigation was realised using the thermocycler with the realtime  
detection – Lightcycler (Roche) which is the part of Veterinary Research Institute laboratory equipment. The specific primers and probe for the detection of gene for parvalbumin of Atlantic mackerel were designed. Moreover the primers and probe for the  
identification of the vertebrates DNA as the amplification control of DNA isolated have been  
designed. The method was verified investigating of the first thirty one samples of commercial canned mackerel products in the retail market of the Czech Republic. Allergenic parvalbumin was detected in 16 food products in conformity with the declaration. Seven samples were negative in spite of the declaration and in four samples DNA amplification was not possible 14 probably based on its complete degradation via technological processes. The method will be granted to the State Administration to secure the food of animal origin control.
